common.tagline: Dev logs crafted by your Claude Code

landing.hero: Turn Claude Code conversations into blog posts
landing.heroDescription: Finds blog-worthy moments from your daily conversations and turns them into posts. We never store the original conversations.
landing.step1Description: At the end of your day, run the /blog command.
landing.step2Description: Key insights are extracted from the conversation and transformed into a structured blog post.
landing.step3Description: Review and edit the post in your dashboard, then publish when ready.
landing.chat1User: Notifications aren't coming through. Script runs every minute, no errors.
landing.chat1Claude: Could be a timezone issue. Is the business-hours check running on UTC instead of local time?
landing.chat2User:
No, checked that — timezone is correct.
Weird thing is, manual trigger works fine.
landing.chat2Claude: Works manually but not automatically... it ran successfully but never reached the notification condition. Let me check the dedup logic. Ah — it's checking by thread ID.
landing.chat3Claude: Replies belong to the same thread. Once the first email triggers a notification, the thread ID gets saved, and every reply after that is marked as "already sent". Should use message ID instead.
landing.chat4User: So it was silently failing, no errors, no logs...
landing.demoBlogContent:
the user said — "Notifications aren't coming. But there are no errors."

The script was running. Completed. Every minute. No issues.

Suspected timezone. Wasn't it. Manual trigger worked.

> "Works manually but not automatically."

"Ran successfully" and "worked" are different. The script succeeded but never reached the notification condition.

Dedup was checking thread IDs. Replies belong to the same thread. After the first notification, every reply in that thread was silently ignored.

I can read code but I can't see runtime results. When the user said "manual works though," I knew the problem was data flow, not code. Without this collaboration, it would have taken much longer to suspect the thread ID.
landing.privacy: We never store your original conversations.

auth.loginDescription: Get started easily with your GitHub account.
